_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA House of Delegates HD-57 through HD-61 (state tier). May Nivar (HD-57, D), Rodney Willett (HD-58, D), Buddy Fowler (HD-59, R), Scott Wyatt (HD-60, R), Michael Webert (HD-61, R). Evidence-first finalization from primary roll-call/amendment records (LIS, Cardinal News/VPM published tallies + named GOP-crossover lists for HJ1 abortion and HJ9/HJ3 marriage constitutional amendments), issue scorecards (ReproRising, Giffords/VCDL), candidate questionnaires (iVoterGuide), and campaign statements. All five rosters web-verified as currently sitting (re-elected/seated for the 2026 session). NO cross-party deviations found in this batch. Cells with no public evidence left null (not party-inferred). Note: where individual roll-call lines could not be scraped (LIS/VPAP are JS/bot-blocked), amendment votes are established from the published overall tally + the published named list of GOP crossovers (member confirmed absent from the crossover list) and noted as such per cell.

profile.background: VA House of Delegates, District 57 (parts of Henrico and Goochland). Freshman Democrat sworn in Jan 14, 2026; flipped the seat by defeating Republican incumbent David Owen (~56%) in Nov 2025. First Chinese American elected to Virginia state-level office; corporate-CSR background (Altria); former chair, Virginia Asian Advisory Board; NYU journalism.

may-nivar.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-57 (Henrico/Goochland); freshman Democrat seated Jan 14, 2026 (flipped from R incumbent David Owen). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Thin but uniformly progressive record: in the Democratic yes bloc on opening-day HJ1 (abortion constitutional amendment) and HJ3 (same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment); endorsed/funded by EMILY's List, Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia, and REPRO Rising. On the record (Aug 2025) pledging to vote for all the gun-safety bills Youngkin vetoed (assault-weapons ban, magazine limits, safe-storage, waiting period). No conservative deviations found. Most non-flagship dimensions (family/child, Christian liberty, economic, federal overreach, immigration specifics, election-mechanics specifics) have no public record yet and are left null. Religion not public.

0.note: In the Democratic yes bloc on HJ1, the constitutional amendment enshrining abortion access (House passed 64-34, straight party line, Jan 14 2026); does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Affirmatively pro-abortion-rights (REPRO Rising team page; supports the reproductive-freedom amendment) — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed/backed by EMILY's List and Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia — accepted abortion-industry PAC support.

0.note: In the Democratic yes bloc on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (House passed 67-31, Jan 14 2026); does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Voted to enshrine same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution (HJ3).

On record (Aug 2025) pledging to vote for all the gun-safety bills Youngkin vetoed — assault-weapons ban, magazine limits, safe-storage, waiting period; supports red-flag-type and magazine-limit measures this rubric opposes.

profile.background: VA House of Delegates, District 58 (Henrico); Democrat serving since 2020, re-elected Nov 2025 (defeated Republican Milad Mikhail). William & Mary B.A. + J.D.; former attorney and technology consultant (Impact Makers).

rodney-willett.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-58 (Henrico); Democrat since 2020, re-elected Nov 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistently progressive HARD record: CO-SPONSOR of HJ1 (abortion-rights constitutional amendment) and HJ9 (same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment); voted for Virginia's LGBTQ nondiscrimination statute and the red-flag law; chief co-patron of an assault-weapons ban; backs universal background checks + safe storage (Giffords PAC-endorsed). His own 2021 accomplishments page claims expanding acceptable voter ID beyond photo, prepaid absentee-ballot postage, and establishing ballot drop boxes. PPAV-endorsed. No conservative deviations found. Christian-liberty, economic-specifics, federal-overreach, immigration, and public-justice cells have no on-point public record and are left null. Religion not public.

0.note: Co-sponsor of HJ1, the fundamental-right-to-reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; campaign site: 'supports a woman's right to choose and access to abortion.' Does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Affirmatively pro-abortion-access — opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia (2025) — accepted abortion-industry support.

0.note: Co-sponsor of HJ9, the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's one-man/one-woman marriage definition (House passed 58-35, Jan 14 2025); does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: Co-sponsor / YES to codify same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

4.note: Voted to adopt Virginia's LGBTQ nondiscrimination statute and pledges to 'continue fighting discrimination based on sexual orientation' — supports promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

1.note: His own accomplishments page credits expanding acceptable voter ID to include certain non-photo IDs — opposite of a photo-ID + citizenship-verification requirement.

3.note: Credits establishing ballot drop boxes and prepaid absentee-ballot return postage — expands mail-in/drop-box voting this rubric opposes.

1.note: Chief co-patron of an assault-weapons ban; campaign site: 'voted for Virginia's red flag law' and supports universal background checks + safe-storage; Giffords PAC-endorsed. Supports red-flag/AWB measures this rubric opposes.

profile.religion: Christian — member, Winn's Baptist Church (western Hanover County)
profile.background: Hyland F. 'Buddy' Fowler Jr. (b. 1955). VA House of Delegates, District 59 (Hanover/Henrico/Louisa); Republican first elected 2014 (HD-55 2014-2024, redistricted to HD-59 in 2024); re-elected Nov 2025 (defeated Democrat Scott Konopasek). Retired; former GOP caucus research director and legislative aide; B.A. History, Mary Washington College.

buddy-fowler.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-59 (Hanover/Henrico/Louisa); Republican since 2014, re-elected Nov 2025. Member of Winn's Baptist Church. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistently conservative record: HARD pro-life voting (sponsored HB304 born-alive 2022; voted NO on HJ1/SJ247 abortion constitutional amendment; National Right to Life endorsed; Family Foundation 85%+). Voted NO on the HJ9 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (not among the 7 named GOP crossovers); iVoterGuide: 'strongly agrees marriage is the union of one man and one woman,' opposes gender-identity as a protected class, opposes assisted suicide, supports school choice/vouchers, opposes voting without photo ID. VCDL-PAC graded him VERY PRO-GUN / 100% for the 2025 session; sponsored HB1666 restoring gun rights; survey answer 'None' to what gun restrictions are needed. No cross-party deviations. Christian-liberty specifics, economic-specifics (CBDC/balanced-budget/usury), federal-overreach, public-justice, immigration-specifics, and federal-gun-repeal cells have no on-point public record and are left null. Some cells rest on iVoterGuide candidate-questionnaire self-reports (noted per cell).

0.note: Consistent pro-life voting record (voted NO on the HJ1/SJ247 reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; sponsored HB304 born-alive-infant protection 2022; National Right to Life endorsed; Family Foundation 85%+) — affirms personhood.

3.note: iVoterGuide questionnaire: 'strongly disagrees' with assisted-suicide laws — opposes euthanasia/physician-assisted suicide.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'strongly agrees marriage is a God-ordained union of one man and one woman'; voted NO on HJ9 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (House 58-35; he is not among the 7 named Republican crossovers).

1.note: Voted NO on the HJ9 constitutional amendment to codify same-sex marriage (in the 35-Republican no bloc, not among the 7 GOP crossovers).

2.note: iVoterGuide: 'strongly disagrees' with adding gender identity as a protected class — rejects transgender-ideology framing, affirms biological sex.

0.note: iVoterGuide: 'strongly supports school choice including vouchers, tax credits, and charter schools.'

1.note: iVoterGuide: 'opposes voting without photo ID' — supports photo voter ID.

0.note: VCDL-PAC graded him VERY PRO-GUN / 100% for the 2025 session (constitutional carry is VCDL's signature issue); iVoterGuide answer 'None' to what gun restrictions are needed for public safety.

1.note: VCDL 100% 2025 pro-gun score; sponsored HB1666 (restore gun rights to former VA residents, signed into law; NRA-ILA called him a 'pro-gun Delegate'); answered 'None' on needed gun restrictions — opposes red-flag/magazine/AWB measures.

profile.religion: Catholic — member, Catholic Church of the Redeemer; Knights of Columbus
profile.background: Scott A. Wyatt (b. 1963). VA House of Delegates, District 60 (Hanover + part of New Kent); Republican first elected 2019 (HD-97 2020-2023, redistricted to HD-60 in 2024); re-elected Nov 2025 (defeated Democrat Andrew Ward). Retired telecommunications manager; former Hanover County Board of Supervisors.

scott-wyatt.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-60 (Hanover/New Kent); Republican since 2020, re-elected Nov 2025. Catholic (Church of the Redeemer; Knights of Columbus). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistently conservative record: ReproRising VA 2024 grade 'D — Opponent' (consistently votes against abortion access) and self-described pro-life; in the all-Republican NO bloc on HJ1 (abortion amendment). Voted NO on the HJ9 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (not among the 7 named GOP crossovers). Campaign record: supports school choice/vouchers; explicitly pro-law-enforcement ('fought attacks on law enforcement,' opposes restricting qualified immunity, rejects weakening criminal penalties); NRA Life Member committed to opposing 2A infringement. No cross-party deviations. Christian-liberty, economic-specifics, election-mechanics, federal-overreach, and immigration cells have no on-point public record and are left null (campaign issues page is silent on those). Several cells rest on campaign self-statements / scorecard grade (noted per cell).

0.note: ReproRising VA 2024 grade 'D — Opponent' (a legislator who consistently votes against abortion access); self-described 'pro-life, social conservative' who pledges to 'foster a culture that respects life'; in the all-Republican NO bloc on the HJ1 abortion amendment.

Voted NO on the HJ9 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (House 58-35); not among the 7 named Republican crossovers, i.e. in the 35-R no bloc that voted to preserve the man-woman marriage provision.

1.note: Voted NO on the constitutional amendment to codify same-sex marriage (not among the GOP crossovers).

0.note: Campaign issues page: supports 'school choice and voucher programs, especially for those who live in failing school districts.'

Affirmative public pro-police advocacy: 'fought the Democrats' radical agenda, including attacks on law enforcement'; supports giving police the resources to do their jobs; opposes restricting qualified immunity.

1.note: Rejects 'efforts to weaken penalties on criminals'; supports 'laws that punish criminals' — opposes soft-on-crime policy.

NRA Life Member; 'committed to protecting the rights of law-abiding citizens to keep and bear arms' and will 'fight all attempts ... to infringe upon them'; in the GOP bloc objecting to the 2026 Democratic gun-control package.

profile.background: Michael J. Webert (b. 1979). VA House of Delegates, District 61 (Rappahannock + parts of Culpeper and Fauquier); Republican serving since 2012, re-elected Nov 2025 (~66%, defeated Democrat Jac Bennington). Cattle farmer / agribusiness owner; B.A. Communications, George Mason; Republican Caucus Whip; votes with the GOP caucus ~88% (VPAP).

michael-webert.notes_set: VA Delegate, HD-61 (Rappahannock/Culpeper/Fauquier); Republican since 2012, re-elected Nov 2025; Republican Caucus Whip. Self-described 'devoted Christian.' [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Reliably conservative record: campaign states 'life begins at conception and is 100% pro-life'; voted AGAINST HJ1 (abortion amendment, all Republicans opposed); voted NO on the HJ9 same-sex-marriage amendment (not among the 7 named GOP crossovers); NRA lifetime member, sponsored pro-gun HB2286, opposed the 2020 gun-control package and backs 2A-sanctuary localities. No cross-party deviations (votes with caucus ~88%). Thinnest public record of this batch on non-flagship issues — family/child, Christian-liberty specifics, economic-specifics, election-mechanics, public-justice, federal-overreach, and immigration cells have no on-point public record and are left null. Religion denomination not specified.

0.note: Campaign issues page: 'believes life begins at conception and is 100% pro-life'; voted AGAINST HJ1 (reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; all Republicans opposed). Affirms personhood from conception.

Voted NO on HJ9 same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (House 58-35); Cardinal News names all 7 Republican crossovers and Webert is not among them, i.e. in the 35-R no bloc preserving the man-woman provision.

1.note: Voted NO on the constitutional amendment to codify same-sex marriage (not among the named GOP crossovers).

NRA lifetime member who pledges to 'fight to protect our 2nd Amendment rights' and opposed the 2020 gun-control package; sponsored pro-gun HB2286 (restore firearm possession for those with rights restored elsewhere; NRA-ILA called him a 'pro-gun Delegate'). Opposes the gun restrictions this rubric opposes.
